operating_state: Total net sales increased 8.6% to $155.7 billion driven by AWS growth of 16.9% and North America segment expansion, while International growth of 4.9% faced foreign exchange headwinds. Consolidated operating income rose 20.2% to $18.4 billion with AWS operating income expanding 22.6% and both North America and International segments delivering double-digit operating income growth driven by increased customer usage and advertising revenue expansion.
cost_structure: Cost of sales improved to 49.4% of revenue from 50.7% benefiting from fulfillment network efficiencies and lower transportation costs despite increased product and shipping costs. Technology and infrastructure expenses increased driven by AWS infrastructure expansion and accelerated depreciation from shortened server and networking equipment useful lives from six to five years reflecting artificial intelligence technology development.
material_events: Converted portion of Anthropic convertible notes to nonvoting preferred stock recognizing $3.3 billion gain in other income. Increased commercial paper program capacity from $20.0 billion to $30.0 billion in April 2025. Changed depreciation estimates for servers and networking equipment from six to five years effective January 2025 reflecting accelerated technology development.
macro_exposures: Foreign exchange exposure intensified with $18.6 billion in foreign-denominated cash creating potential losses of $930 million to $3.7 billion under adverse rate scenarios. China regulatory and trade restrictions pose escalating risks given significant China-based seller revenue and supplier dependencies. Increased regulatory scrutiny across multiple jurisdictions with ongoing litigation including price fixing, monopolization, and consumer protection claims.
forward_outlook: AWS performance obligations for contracts exceeding one year totaled approximately $189 billion with weighted-average remaining contract life of 4.1 years. Management expects Q2 2025 net sales between $159.0-164.0 billion representing 7-11% growth with operating income guidance of $13.0-17.5 billion. Committed to invest additional $2.7 billion in Anthropic convertible notes by Q4 2025 while planning sustained investment in artificial intelligence infrastructure.
uncertainty_profile: Income tax contingencies of $6.3 billion remain subject to examination by multiple tax authorities for years 2016 and thereafter. Multiple competition and consumer protection investigations are ongoing regarding store operations, fulfillment practices, Prime services, and AWS offerings with inherently unpredictable outcomes. Complex tax controversies across jurisdictions including contested cloud services tax assessments require resolution with potential for material adjustments exceeding recorded reserves.
